The [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Dagobah Limited">{{CCG|set=Dagobah Limited|cardname=Lieutenant Commander Ardan}}</ref> '''Sinton''' was located in the [[Sinton system/Legends|Sinton system]], a part of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[Rago sector/Legends|Rago sector]].<ref name="Atlas Online Companion" /> It lay along the [[Rago Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]],<ref name="Rago Run" /> which connected it to the [[Moro system/Legends|Moro]] and [[Gilatter system/Legends|Gilatter systems]].<ref name="Atlas" /> Sinton was populated by [[Alien/Legends|non-Humans]],<ref name="Dagobah Limited" /> and at some point by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Sinton date">The [[Subjugation of Sinton]] must have occurred at some point by the events of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], as evidenced by {{CCG|set=Dagobah Limited|cardname=Lieutenant Commander Ardan}}, which is set during the film's events. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of ''The Empire Strikes Back'' to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].</ref> the planet was [[Subjugation of Sinton|subjugated]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]],<ref name="Dagobah Limited" /> which subjected the planet's inhabitants to [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] labor.<ref name="Atlas" />

By [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]],<ref name="Essential Reader's Companion">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of [[Darksaber (novel)|''Darksaber'']], which includes [[Sulamar]] claiming to have participated in the [[Subjugation of Sinton]], to [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]].</ref> [[Sulamar]], a fraudulently self-titled Imperial [[General/Legends|general]], claimed to have participated in the Subjugation of Sinton, among many other purported [[Imperial Military/Legends|military]] conquests<ref name="Darksaber">[[Darksaber (novel)|''Darksaber'']]</ref>—some nonexistent.<ref name="CSWE-Sulamar">{{CSWECite|III|195|Sulamar, General}}</ref> In reality, Sulamar was a low-level Imperial [[Technician/Legends|technician]] with a failed military career.<ref name="Darksaber" /> By that same [[Standard year/Legends|year]], Sinton lay within the borders of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="EGTW" />

==Behind the scenes==
Sinton was first mentioned in the [[1995]] novel [[Darksaber (novel)|''Darksaber'']], written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]], in which the character Imperial General Sulamar alleges to have played a part in the Subjugation of Sinton. Sulamar's claim is later called into doubt once he is exposed as a fraud.<ref name="Darksaber" /> Both the planet and its subjugation were subsequently elaborated upon through the [[1997]] ''[[Dagobah Limited]]'' expansion set of the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]], thus proving Sinton's [[in-universe]] existence.<ref name="Dagobah Limited" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Sinton [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet Sinton, in grid square I-7.<ref name="Atlas" />
